prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real wanted_kr-style job application (iframe-hosted, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox, location-typeahead, repeatable-block. Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Reference DOM (Submit outside form): Submit button outside form linked via form= attribute Reference DOM (Datalist autocomplete): Location field uses input list= with datalist suggestions Reference DOM (Role group not fieldset): Grouped radios use div role=group instead of fieldset Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: pill-yes-no, div-role-textbox, shadow-host, decoy-form, iframe-apply. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: unstyled div soup with inline styles only. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Submit outside form; Datalist autocomplete; Role group not fieldset.
